title: [lichen_l2.c:838-841] lichen_l2_ctx struct empty but required by NET_DEVICE_INIT
description:
Gap found: L2 context type pattern not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Boilerplate struct considered trivial.

The actual bug: Lines 838-841 define an empty struct lichen_l2_ctx which is required by NET_DEVICE_INIT. The comment says 'Required by NET_DEVICE_INIT; actual state is module-static'. This is correct but:

1. The empty struct wastes 1 byte per interface (C requires structs to have non-zero size)
2. If future refactoring needs per-interface state, the pattern of using module-static vars will need to change
3. Zephyr's NET_L2_GET_CTX_TYPE_* macro (line 844) ties the context type to the L2 name

This is a design pattern observation, not a bug. The current approach works for single-interface LICHEN deployments. Multi-interface would require rethinking the static state model. Priority 4 - architecture note.
close_reason: Fixed: Added explanatory comment for empty lichen_l2_ctx struct - documents that it is intentionally empty because Zephyr's NET_DEVICE_INIT requires a context type, but LICHEN uses module-static state instead of per-interface context
